[2018-05-23 09:40] liadahlia (Lia): rush tia faster :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-05-23 09:40] gormo. (Gorm): How is auric impulse meant to be used?
[2018-05-23 09:41] gormo. (Gorm): Seems like a strictly worse drone
[2018-05-23 09:41] liadahlia (Lia): you buy them instead of drones and then stop buying them when you want to "sell" them
[2018-05-23 09:41] liadahlia (Lia): it's very much better than drones
[2018-05-23 09:41] liadahlia (Lia): the skill here is that you need to predict when you would want to sell your drone
[2018-05-23 09:41] liadahlia (Lia): and then buy it a few turns early
[2018-05-23 09:42] gormo. (Gorm): For a one turn cash boost?
[2018-05-23 09:42] liadahlia (Lia): it's not a cash boost
[2018-05-23 09:42] gormo. (Gorm): Well, true since you can just save cash
[2018-05-23 09:42] liadahlia (Lia): think about all those situations where you float 3 gold and you want to spend them
[2018-05-23 09:42] liadahlia (Lia): spending it on drones might not be the best
[2018-05-23 09:42] liadahlia (Lia): drones take quite a while to have positive returns
[2018-05-23 09:43] gormo. (Gorm): So it's only better if you think a drone will live less than 4 turns
[2018-05-23 09:43] altafen (Altafen#6776): when do i buy isokronus to sync them?
[2018-05-23 09:43] gormo. (Gorm): "Live" as in "provide useful gold"
[2018-05-23 09:43] liadahlia (Lia): in situations where you need money now now now it's better to buy auric
[2018-05-23 09:43] liadahlia (Lia): anyways, if it makes it easier for you you can think of it as a convenient way to spend floated gold
[2018-05-23 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): though better to think of it as a drone that you can sell any time in exchange for 1 energy per turn
[2018-05-23 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): i wouldn't say it's "So it's only better if you think a drone will live less than 4 turns"
[2018-05-23 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): in a lot of cases buying more drones is just bad
[2018-05-23 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): or rather, suboptimal
[2018-05-23 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): let's say you're in a game with high inflation, not necessarily over in 4 turns
[2018-05-23 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): but a game where that 3 gold might be better contributed towards a shadowfang or something
[2018-05-23 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): then auric is way better, because instead of floating 3 gold or buying a drone that you might not need, you get 4 gold towards a shadowfang the next turn
[2018-05-23 09:46] liadahlia (Lia): what do you mean @Altafen#6776 ?
[2018-05-23 09:46] gormo. (Gorm): What is mean by high inflation?
[2018-05-23 09:46] liadahlia (Lia): generally syncing isos is good as absorb denial, otherwise isos are generally pretty bad
[2018-05-23 09:47] liadahlia (Lia): yeah sorry for "high inflation", it's a pretty ambiguous term people use around here
[2018-05-23 09:48] liadahlia (Lia): in this case i mean it as "there are things that generate much better value than a gold per turn"
[2018-05-23 09:48] liadahlia (Lia): often when you have more attack than opponent have absorb, each sword is worth much more than 1 gold
[2018-05-23 09:48] liadahlia (Lia): at that point you would want to stop buying drones, because there are better things that generate more value
[2018-05-23 09:49] altafen (Altafen#6776): specifically it was "which turn do i buy new ones when i already have some on the table because i haven't done this before" but i guessed and it was right
[2018-05-23 09:49] liadahlia (Lia): buy them on turns you would buy normal attackers
[2018-05-23 09:50] gormo. (Gorm): What do I want to do when my opponent opens double dynamo
[2018-05-23 09:50] gormo. (Gorm): How do I take advantage of their greed
[2018-05-23 09:50] altafen (Altafen#6776): was asking about the tactics, not the strategy - but ~~i'm good now~~
nvm i messed it up the second time
[2018-05-23 09:51] liadahlia (Lia): as p1 against a double dynamo, a standard dynamo opening, you just open with your own standard dynamo
[2018-05-23 09:51] gormo. (Gorm): There are perforators in the set as the only aggro option, except for tarsier
[2018-05-23 09:51] liadahlia (Lia): it's not that much worse
[2018-05-23 09:51] liadahlia (Lia): there's this opening you can go called evil animus
[2018-05-23 09:51] liadahlia (Lia): where you go animus after your first dynamo instead of 2 drones
[2018-05-23 09:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): you buy auric when 4 is better than drone and have a spare 3E
[2018-05-23 09:51] liadahlia (Lia): which lets you build 2 red units the next turn and pressure him
[2018-05-23 09:52] gormo. (Gorm): Is evil animus considered superior to standard dynamo?
[2018-05-23 09:52] liadahlia (Lia): but that would require red units that can attack immediately, which are grimbotches or vores
[2018-05-23 09:52] p0lari (Polari): you also buy auric instead of a drone if you know next turn you'll have spare energy and won't otherwise be buying a drone
[2018-05-23 09:52] p0lari (Polari): you gain option value and lose nothing
[2018-05-23 09:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's a relatively low impact unit in the grand scheme of things but incredible utility/flex
[2018-05-23 09:52] liadahlia (Lia): it's better than standard p1 dynamo lines in some sets

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-05-23) ---
[2018-05-23 09:52] liadahlia (Lia): particularly aggressive sets with low absorb
[2018-05-23 09:52] p0lari (Polari): it's sometimes fairly high impact when it lets you rush out big purchases
[2018-05-23 09:53] p0lari (Polari): like with odin or lucy or antima
[2018-05-23 09:53] gormo. (Gorm): And what are the determinants for going triple engineer?
[2018-05-23 09:54] gormo. (Gorm): I assume high unit costs and good defenders?
[2018-05-23 09:54] liadahlia (Lia): high absorb, high soak units
[2018-05-23 09:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): evil animus generally relies on a fast red attacker to work
[2018-05-23 09:54] gormo. (Gorm): What's the difference between absorb and soak
[2018-05-23 09:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): botch, vore, maybe smorcus
[2018-05-23 09:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): absorb is defense you get every turn
[2018-05-23 09:54] p0lari (Polari): you should understand the basic dynamics of why prismata games develop the way they do
[2018-05-23 09:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): soak is defense you have to pay for every time you want it
[2018-05-23 09:54] p0lari (Polari): attack is generally more efficient than econ
[2018-05-23 09:55] p0lari (Polari): but absorb is op and nullifies the first attackers
[2018-05-23 09:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): generally. the slowest sets occur when econ is more efficient than attack, due to the presence of strong soak (like aegis and plexo and poly), but no strong attack
[2018-05-23 09:55] liadahlia (Lia): to put it differently, soak is health that you can buy, often prompt defense, absorb is usually units that have high blue health and can block
[2018-05-23 09:55] p0lari (Polari): so if you start rushing attack right away, the opponent gets some econ and then an absorber, which sort of resets the game to both having no attack but him having higher econ
[2018-05-23 09:56] p0lari (Polari): the better the defence and especially absorb in the set, and the worse the attack, the higher the econ where you can break even this way

[2018-05-23 20:27] amalloy: so, tarsier+wall costs 9BR, and produces 3 prompt soak plus a slow attacker. compare this with blood pact + splitter, which also costs 9BR, and produces 3 prompt soak (ignoring the 1 husk your opponent's grimbotch kills), plus an immediate attacker. the two plays seem very similar: the husk plan gives you less value long-term, but more value short-term. am i right that at lower inflations TW is preferable, and at high enough inflation S1 is better? how can i tell when it's time to switch?
[2018-05-23 20:28] amalloy: of course, if you're under enough pressure, then W1 is better than either play, but there must be some midpoint where S1 is a better compromise

[2018-05-23 20:29] Deleted User (Deleted User): so u switch when wall supply is gfed

[2018-05-23 20:39] p0lari (Polari): on your next turn you have one more attack and need one more defence
[2018-05-23 20:39] p0lari (Polari): on the turn after that the opponent has 2 extra soak

- Is it preferable to rush zomorra or lucina?
- Is scorchilla rush better than either?

[2018-05-23 22:33] leary93: and scorchzilla is too slow to pressure out lucina, but is a fine transition to get after I think

[2018-05-23 22:35] serchiot: 8conduits is considered good support for zemorra
[2018-05-23 22:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): there's not much support in this set, like poly is the only defnsive unit here
[2018-05-23 22:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): scorch and zem both might as well not be in the set
[2018-05-23 22:36] leary93: Isnt poly pretty decent to get lucina out?
[2018-05-23 22:36] leary93: like its good enough to stop the intial tarsiers
[2018-05-23 22:36] masn6811 (Masne98888): the tarsier player has better tech for a luci transition
[2018-05-23 22:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): the slow animus in paticular is excellent at punishing DDE here

[2018-05-23 22:37] leary93: I mustve missed something

[2018-05-23 22:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): like the term "lucina rush" is kinda vague
[2018-05-23 22:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): but it must refer to either one player or the other
[2018-05-23 22:38] amalloy: you say polywall is "good enough to stop the intial tarsiers", but polywall isn't that amazing early against tarsier spam. if you build it as your first defender, you get 2 absorb, then 4 absorb, then 0 absorb. not really better on average than wall. it's good soak, but that's more important later than early
[2018-05-23 22:38] leary93: I honestly just ment get lucina as your first attacker
[2018-05-23 22:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): and hope it doesn't run into a poly? :LongNoseLiar:

[2018-05-23 22:39] leary93: I mean, ye lucina kinda stings if she runs into poly
[2018-05-23 22:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): and i gave a comment on p1, and a comment on p2
[2018-05-23 22:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): put together, this is a generic answer
[2018-05-23 22:39] leary93: you are right though, thinking about it
[2018-05-23 22:39] leary93: 2 tarsiers are pretty useful even for getting lucina out
[2018-05-23 22:39] leary93: because you hit that number 6
[2018-05-23 22:41] gormo. (Gorm): So if we go tarsiers into lucina, and opponent builds polywalls into zomorra
[2018-05-23 22:42] gormo. (Gorm): Do we survive post-zomorra turns? Do we get that far?
[2018-05-23 22:44] awaclus: you can usually survive a couple of Zemora turns, but mostly the intent is that your opponent can't survive long enough to activate Zemora very many times in the first place if you're playing non-mirror vs. Zemora
[2018-05-23 22:44] leary93: you have to understand, that there isn't any support, so no way to spend green, and you still have to get enough green to get to fire Zemora. That means getting at least 3-4 conduits, and building more after Zemora starts firing. That's a lot of gold invested that doesnt immediatly pay off vs a big red strat
